title: Ops: Lifecycle & Health
description: Manage operational lifecycle events, run health checks, and handle trigger conditions for the Evolver loop process.

actors.name: Operator / CLI
actors.description: Human or automated caller invoking lifecycle commands via CLI (node lifecycle.js [action]) or programmatic API

actors.name: Evolver Loop Process
actors.description: The detached child node process spawned and managed by the lifecycle module (--loop flag)

actors.name: Wrapper / External Caller
actors.description: External wrapper script (feishu-evolver-wrapper) or ops/index consumer that calls trigger.send() to wake the loop

actors.name: Operating System
actors.description: Provides process signals (SIGTERM/SIGKILL), file system, disk/memory stats used by health checks

use_cases.name: Start Evolver Loop
use_cases.description: Checks for running PIDs; if none, resolves loop script path (env override → wrapper → core), spawns detached node process with --loop, writes PID file.
use_cases.postcondition: Detached child process running; PID written to memory/evolver_loop.pid

use_cases.name: Stop Evolver Loop
use_cases.description: Finds running PIDs, sends SIGTERM, polls for exit (up to 5s), escalates to SIGKILL if still running, removes PID file and evolver.pid lock.
use_cases.precondition: Evolver loop may or may not be running
use_cases.postcondition: All loop processes terminated; PID files removed

use_cases.name: Restart Evolver Loop
use_cases.description: Calls stop() then start() with a 2000ms delay. Combines UC2 and UC1.

use_cases.name: Query Loop Status
use_cases.description: Returns running state, list of PIDs with their command lines, and log file path.

use_cases.name: Tail Log Output
use_cases.description: Reads the last N lines (default 20) from the evolver log file and returns content.

use_cases.name: Check Process Health
use_cases.description: Verifies loop is running and log file has been updated within MAX_SILENCE_MS. Returns healthy/unhealthy with reason (not_running | stagnation).
use_cases.extension: If unhealthy at CLI: auto-triggers UC3 - Restart Evolver Loop

use_cases.name: Discover Running PIDs
use_cases.description: Scans `ps -e -o pid,args` output for node processes matching --loop + known wrapper paths; filters with isPidRunning(). Internal utility used by start/stop/status/checkHealth.

use_cases.name: Run System Health Check
use_cases.description: Aggregates multiple system checks and returns overall status (ok | warning | error) plus per-check results.
sub_checks: process_count via /proc readdir (Linux only, cached 60s) — warning >2000

use_cases.name: Resolve Loop Script Path
use_cases.description: Priority: EVOLVER_LOOP_SCRIPT env var → skills/feishu-evolver-wrapper/index.js if exists → repo root index.js.

use_cases.name: Send Wake Trigger
use_cases.description: Writes 'WAKE' string to memory/evolver_wake.signal file to immediately wake a polling wrapper.
use_cases.postcondition: Signal file exists; wrapper will detect and act

use_cases.name: Check Trigger Pending
use_cases.description: Returns true if the wake signal file exists. Used by wrapper to poll for pending triggers.

use_cases.name: Clear Wake Trigger
use_cases.description: Removes the wake signal file after it has been consumed.

key_relationships: ops/index.js aggregates lifecycle, trigger, skillsMonitor, cleanup, commentary, selfRepair
checkHealth (lifecycle) extends to restart if unhealthy (CLI 'check' command)
start (lifecycle) uses getLoopScript for dynamic script resolution
stop (lifecycle) escalates SIGTERM → SIGKILL with polling
important_notes: MAX_SILENCE_MS from config controls stagnation detection threshold in checkHealth()
PID file: memory/evolver_loop.pid; lock file: <repoRoot>/evolver.pid — both cleaned on stop
Wake signal file: memory/evolver_wake.signal — written by trigger.send(), polled by wrapper
health_check.js is standalone (no lifecycle dependency); can be called independently
Process count check (/proc) is Linux-only and cached for 60 seconds to reduce overhead
FEISHU_APP_ID/SECRET missing is downgraded to 'warning' (not critical) to prevent restart loops
start() augments PATH with ~/.npm-global/bin for the spawned child environment
